title: teucrium chamaedrys subsp. pectinatum
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. pectinatum, also known as Germander or Wood Germander, is a subspecies of the Germander plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family of herbs. Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. pectinatum has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting liver function. Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. pectinatum can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies.
